title: Brachypterum scandens
taxonomy: Brachypterum scandens is a species of flowering plant in the family Restionaceae. It is native to New Zealand. The plant's classification places it in the kingdom Plantae, further classified into the phylum Angiospermophyta, and then into the class Liliopsida. Within the class Liliopsida, Brachypterum scandens is further classified into the order Poales and then into the family Restionaceae. The plant's species classification places it within the genus Brachypterum.

intro: Brachypterum scandens, also known as Chilean valerian, is a flowering plant native to the Andes mountain range in South America. The health benefits of Brachypterum scandens include its use as a natural sedative, anti-anxiety agent, and muscle relaxant, making it a popular remedy for insomnia and stress relief. The bioactive constituents of this plant include valeranone, valerenic acid, and valerenal, which are responsible for its sedative and anxiolytic properties. Brachypterum scandens is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often consumed to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
